mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative equity repricing within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 50% across multiple economic cycles, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, causing short-term equity sell-offs within 5 trading days during high-inflation environments.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO falls below its 12-month average during high-inflation periods, or the signal shows zero predictive power in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real interest rate compression, which boosts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate compression signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows, or 20-day returns do not outperform baseline during periods of falling real rates.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth rates, or the signal generates a negative cumulative alpha over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.5% signals economic stagnation and reduced corporate profit expectations, triggering immediate negative equity repricing within 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or the threshold signal fails to achieve statistical significance in event-study analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during disinflationary periods indicates healthy supply-side capacity meeting demand without triggering price pressures, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations when INDPRO rises during disinflationary regimes, or the regime-filtered signal shows zero predictive accuracy.
